_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Mandible Tumour Resection and reconstruction / Cancer surgery
source_files: Surgery for Cyst & Tumor of Maxilla, Mandible _PPD.CPD.pdf
Surgery for Cyst & Tumor of Maxilla, Mandible_MEDICO.pdf
clinical_indications: Mandible tumour requiring resection and reconstruction
Advanced or radical treatment for cyst/tumour of mandible not amenable to conservative techniques like enucleation or marsupialization

preauth.label: Clinical notes (detailing signs, symptoms, examination findings, clinical photographs (intraoral and extraoral), indications for doing the procedure & advise for admission)
Investigation reports of Cyst/Tumour (X-ray for Odontogenic cyst OR CBCT/CT of Jaws OR Intraoral Radiograph AND/OR OPG-Orthopantomography) to confirm existence

claim.label: Procedure note/ operative note & Anesthesia notes (where applicable)
Post-procedure Investigation reports of Cyst/Tumour (X-ray for Odontogenic cyst OR Intraoral Radiograph &/ or OPG-Orthopantomography for existence & Histopathology report, Biopsy or FNAC)

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Oral maxillofacial Clinical notes (including clinical signs & symptoms such as disfigurement of face difficulty in breathing, examination findings, indications for doing the procedure & advise for admission)?
X-ray report of cyst/tumour of Maxilla/Mandible and Biopsy /FNAC of cyst and tumour of Oral maxillofacial region?

cpd_claim.q: Indoor case papers & informed written consent Submitted?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up?

Are the Investigation reports of of Cyst/Tumour such as: X-ray for Odontogenic cyst Or Intraoral Radiograph &/ or OPG-Orthopantomography to confirm the existence & Histopathology report, Biopsy or FNAC Submitted?

common_queries: Signs, symptoms, examination, or X-ray do not confirm the presence of Cyst or Tumour in Maxilla/Mandible.
X-Ray/OPG-Orthopantomography /Biopsy/FNAC does not confirm Cyst or Tumour.
Detailed Oral maxillofacial Clinical notes are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization stage.
X-ray report of cyst/tumour of Maxilla/Mandible or Biopsy /FNAC of cyst and tumour of Oral maxillofacial region are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization stage.
Indoor case papers or informed written consent are missing at claim submission stage.
Documentation for appropriate post-op care or follow-up advice is missing at claim submission stage.
Barcode of Implant(s) used is missing at claim submission stage.
Post-procedure Investigation reports (Histopathology, Biopsy, FNAC) or imaging reports are missing at claim submission stage.

clinical_key_pointers: Cyst of jaw, oral and facial soft tissue can be Intraosseous cyst (odontogenic or non-odontogenic in origin) and soft tissue cyst.
The keratocystic odontogenic tumor is back into the cyst category as odontogenic keratocyst (OKC).
Depending on the size of the cyst, its location and the patients' age, several treatment options are available: curettage, enucleation, radical treatment and marsupialization.

The marsupialization /enucleation /excision/ Reconstruction/resection for cyst and tumour in maxilla and mandible is a conservative technique used in early tumor stages, as curative treatment for the odontogenic cyst.
Despite its disadvantages, the marsupialization remains an interesting therapeutic choice in the case of large cysts, or in very young or old patients.
Odontogenic cysts are usually treated by enucleation (cystectomy).
Limited cysts (less than 5 cm) are usually managed by primary excision (total cystectomy), whereas larger ones (exceeding 5 cm) are often decompressed or marsupialized.

Decompression consists only of opening a much smaller surgical window, associating the creation of an opening (window) into the cystic cavity with/without the suturing of a decompressing device /gauge pack.
No specific symptoms of cyst and tumor in jaw, usually associated with carious and non-vital tooth.
Worrisome mouth ulcers, swellings and abnormally colored areas of the gums and lining tissues of the mouth may arise.
Warning signs can include ulcers, white patches, mixed red and white patches, or red patches inside your mouth or on your lips. These patches can often become malignant.
The cause of Cyst & Tumour is unknown, however some are associated with genetic syndromes.
People with nevoid basal cell carcinoma syndrome (Gorlin-Goltz syndrome) lack a gene that suppresses tumors; the genetic mutation is inherited.
Oral Soft tissue Swelling, bone pain, numbness, tenderness, and unexplained tooth mobility can be symptoms.
Benign tumors and cysts can cause damage to surrounding bone and tissue.
contraindications: Marsupialization is contraindicated for limited cystic lesions that require continuous drainage.
Marsupialization is contraindicated when previous conservative treatment failed and when complete or marginal resection is indicated.
Marsupialization is contraindicated for cysts with tumour potential (KCOC/KEROTOCYST).
Enucleation is contraindicated for eruptional cyst, friable cyst, and large cyst where surgical access would weaken the jaw potentially leading to fracture.
